message:
test(robot): add bv integration E2E test

Add new E2E test for robot-bv integration testing the interaction
between ntm robot mode and beads viewer commands.

Also sync beads issue tracker state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ntm-hgl9","title":"Add ntm workflow CLI commands","description":"## Overview
Add CLI commands to manage and run workflows.

## Location
Create `internal/cli/workflow.go`

## Commands

### ntm workflow list
```bash
ntm workflow list
ntm workflow list --json
```
Output:
```
WORKFLOW          DESCRIPTION                         AGENTS  SOURCE
red-green         TDD with test writer/implementer    2       builtin
specialist-team   Architect, implementer, QA          3       builtin
parallel-explore  Multiple approaches simultaneously  2       builtin
review-pipeline   Code with mandatory review          2       builtin
custom-flow       My custom workflow                  4       project
```

### ntm workflow show <name>
```bash
ntm workflow show red-green
```
Output:
```
Workflow: red-green
Description: Test-Driven Development: write failing tests, then make them pass
Coordination: ping-pong

AGENTS:
  tester (red)       - Writes failing tests that define expected behavior
  implementer (green) - Implements code to make tests pass

FLOW:
  Initial: red
  red -> green (on: test_written)
  green -> red (on: test_passing)

SETUP PROMPTS:
  feature      - What feature are you implementing?
  test_pattern - Test file pattern? [default: *_test.go]
```

### ntm workflow start <name>
```bash
ntm workflow start red-green
ntm workflow start red-green --session=my-tdd
ntm workflow start red-green --feature="user auth" --test_pattern="*_test.go"
```
Interactive mode prompts for setup values.

### ntm workflow status
```bash
ntm workflow status [session]
```
Shows current workflow state, stage, recent activity.

### ntm workflow stop
```bash
ntm workflow stop [session]
```
Stops workflow coordination (session continues).

## Flags
- --session: Target session (default: current)
- --json: JSON output
- --dry-run: Show what would happen

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All workflow commands implemented
- [ ] Interactive setup prompts
- [ ] JSON output support
- [ ] Help text and examples
- [ ] Integration tests","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-31T01:17:02.547674475Z","updated_at":"2026-01-19T19:09:41.490237317Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T19:09:41.490183896Z","close_reason":"Dropped: consolidate on recipes/pipelines; no workflow engine","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-hgl9","depends_on_id":"ntm-e58j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"ntm-hgl9","depends_on_id":"ntm-gd05","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"ntm-hgl9","depends_on_id":"ntm-v68e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"ntm-hgl9","depends_on_id":"ntm-z5uy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:09Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-hlek","title":"EPIC: Output Deduplication & Comparison","description":"# Output Deduplication & Comparison

## Background & Motivation

When broadcasting the same prompt to multiple agents, users need to understand:
- Did agents produce similar or different results?
- Which files did each agent modify?
- Are there conflicts (same file, different changes)?

## CRITICAL REFRAME: Focus on FILE Changes, Not Text

Original approach: Compare scrollback text with similarity algorithms.
**Problem**: Scrollback is noisy (tool calls, errors, chatter). Comparing it is not useful.

**Better approach**: Compare WHAT AGENTS PRODUCED (file changes).

## What This Feature Actually Provides

### 1. File Modification Tracking
Track which files each agent modified:
```bash
ntm changes myproject
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ File Changes by Agent                                       │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ src/api/users.py                                            │
│   Modified by: cc_1, cc_2 (potential conflict!)             │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ src/api/auth.py                                             │
│   Modified by: cc_1 only                                    │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ tests/test_users.py                                         │
│   Modified by: cod_1, cod_2                                 │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### 2. Conflict Detection
```bash
ntm conflicts myproject
⚠️ Conflict detected: src/api/users.py
  cc_1: +25 -10 lines (added caching)
  cc_2: +18 -8 lines (added validation)
  
  View diff: ntm diff myproject cc_1 cc_2 src/api/users.py
```

### 3. File-Level Diff
```bash
ntm diff myproject cc_1 cc_2
# Shows git diff between uncommitted changes from each agent
# Uses git stash or worktrees if available
```

### 4. Unique Contributions
```bash
ntm contributions myproject
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Unique Contributions                                        │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ cc_1: src/api/auth.py (only agent to touch this)           │
│ cod_1: tests/integration/* (only agent on tests)           │
│ cod_2: docs/api.md (only agent on docs)                    │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Implementation Approach

### File Change Detection
Option A: Parse git status per pane (if each pane has isolated changes)
Option B: Track file mtimes before/after prompts
Option C: Use git stash snapshots per agent

**Recommended**: Option A/B hybrid
- Snapshot file mtimes before broadcast
- After agents settle, compare to detect changes
- Attribute changes to agents based on timing

### Conflict Detection
```go
type FileChange struct {
    Path      string
    Agent     string
    Timestamp time.Time
    LinesAdded int
    LinesRemoved int
}

func DetectConflicts(changes []FileChange) []Conflict {
    byPath := groupByPath(changes)
    var conflicts []Conflict
    for path, agents := range byPath {
        if len(agents) > 1 {
            conflicts = append(conflicts, Conflict{Path: path, Agents: agents})
        }
    }
    return conflicts
}
```

## What We're NOT Doing

- NOT comparing scrollback text similarity (too noisy)
- NOT semantic analysis of code (too complex)
- NOT LLM-based summarization (expensive, unreliable)

Focus on concrete, observable file changes.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Track file modifications per agent
- [ ] Detect same-file conflicts across agents
- [ ] ntm changes shows file attribution
- [ ] ntm conflicts highlights issues
- [ ] ntm diff shows file-level differences
- [ ] Works with git-based workflows","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-10T08:27:32.763201Z","updated_at":"2025-12-10T22:34:27.468834Z","closed_at":"2025-12-10T22:34:27.468834Z","compaction_level":0}

 {"id":"ntm-hnc9","title":"Write tests for profile system","description":"## Overview
Comprehensive tests for the profile system.

## Location
Create `internal/profile/profile_test.go`

## Test Categories

### Schema Tests
```go
func TestProfileValidation(t *testing.T) {
    // Valid profile
    // Invalid name (spaces, uppercase)
    // Invalid agent type
    // Too long system prompt
    // Invalid glob patterns
}

func TestProfileSetValidation(t *testing.T) {
    // Valid set
    // References non-existent profile
    // Empty profiles list
}
```

### Loader Tests
```go
func TestBuiltinProfiles(t *testing.T) {
    // All 5 builtin profiles load correctly
    // Each has required fields
}

func TestUserProfilesOverride(t *testing.T) {
    // User profile overrides builtin
    // New user profiles added
}

func TestProjectProfilesOverride(t *testing.T) {
    // Project profile overrides user and builtin
    // Precedence is correct
}

func TestProfileNotFound(t *testing.T) {
    // GetProfile returns error for unknown profile
}
```

### Integration Tests
```go
func TestSpawnWithProfiles(t *testing.T) {
    // Spawn session with --profiles flag
    // Verify pane titles include profile names
    // Verify system prompt was sent
}

func TestProfilesListCommand(t *testing.T) {
    // ntm profiles list output
    // JSON output mode
}

func TestProfilesShowCommand(t *testing.T) {
    // ntm profiles show architect
    // Unknown profile error
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unit tests for schema validation
- [ ] Unit tests for loader precedence
- [ ] Integration tests for spawn with profiles
- [ ] Integration tests for CLI commands
- [ ] Test coverage > 80%","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-31T01:10:56.723581115Z","updated_at":"2025-12-31T08:06:56.756279102Z","closed_at":"2025-12-31T08:06:56.756279102Z","close_reason":"Test coverage increased from 53.7% to 91.9%. All acceptance criteria met.","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-hnc9","depends_on_id":"ntm-66cp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-31T01:11:14.025579078Z","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ntm-hnc9","depends_on_id":"ntm-9chh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-31T01:11:14.082098982Z","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ntm-hnc9","depends_on_id":"ntm-j8t0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-31T01:11:12.182399007Z","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ntm-ho9e","title":"Epic: Comprehensive E2E Test Suite with Detailed Logging","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-04T07:42:34.611236Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-01-19T23:03:21.176353501Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T23:03:21.176256459Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":92,"issue_id":"ntm-ho9e","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Epic: Comprehensive E2E Test Suite with Detailed Logging

### Goal
Create a complete end-to-end test suite with shell scripts that provide comprehensive logging for debugging CI failures and understanding test behavior.

### Current State
- 3 E2E test files exist (22 functions)
- Tests use Go's testing framework
- Limited bash/shell scripting for E2E
- Logging exists but could be more structured

### Scope
This epic covers:
1. **Master E2E runner script** - e2e-runner.sh with parallel execution
2. **Structured JSON logging** - Machine-readable logs for each test
3. **Scenario-based scripts** - test-spawn.sh, test-send.sh, test-status.sh
4. **Agent lifecycle tests** - Full spawn -> work -> kill workflows
5. **Robot mode tests** - All --robot-* flag combinations
6. **Multi-session tests** - Concurrent session management

### Key Features of Logging System
- JSON format with timestamps, levels, test names
- Per-test log files plus aggregate summary
- Automatic diagnostic capture on failure
- Timing information for performance tracking
- CI-friendly output format

### Dependencies
- Features depend on this epic
- Tasks depend on runner and logging infrastructure first
- Agent lifecycle tests depend on shell script tests

### Success Criteria
- All E2E scenarios covered with shell scripts
- JSON logs capture complete test execution
- CI can parse logs and report failures
- Diagnostic capture enables debugging without reproduction","created_at":"2026-01-04T07:50:04Z"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-hrdy","title":"Add --robot-dashboard flag and DashboardOutput struct","description":"Add flag to CLI. Create DashboardOutput struct with Fleet, Agents, Beads, Alerts, Metrics fields. Files: internal/cli/root.go, internal/robot/robot.go","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-11T01:55:19.805945Z","updated_at":"2025-12-11T03:26:53.152300Z","closed_at":"2025-12-11T03:26:53.152300Z","compaction_level":0,"comments":[{"id":9,"issue_id":"ntm-hrdy","author":"jemanuel","text":"Implemented --robot-dashboard flag and DashboardOutput. PrintDashboard now emits best-effort JSON: fleet/system info, sessions/agents from tmux, beads summary, alerts, file changes, conflicts, and agent mail summary. Wired flag into CLI run path. Tests: go test ./internal/robot.","created_at":"2025-12-14T04:47:46Z"}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,87 @@
+package e2e
+
+import (
+	"encoding/json"
+	"fmt"
+	"os/exec"
+	"strings"
+	"testing"
+
+	"github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/internal/tools"
+	"github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/tests/testutil"
+)
+
+type robotEnvelope struct {
+	Success   bool   `json:"success"`
+	Timestamp string `json:"timestamp"`
+}
+
+func requireBVVersionAtLeast(t *testing.T, min tools.Version) tools.Version {
+	t.Helper()
+
+	out, err := exec.Command("bv", "--version").CombinedOutput()
+	if err != nil {
+		t.Skipf("bv --version failed: %v", err)
+	}
+	versionText := strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
+	if !tools.VersionRegex.MatchString(versionText) {
+		t.Skipf("bv version not parseable: %q", versionText)
+	}
+	version, _ := tools.ParseStandardVersion(versionText)
+	if !version.AtLeast(min) {
+		t.Skipf("bv version %s < %s", version.String(), min.String())
+	}
+	return version
+}
+
+func assertRobotEnvelope(t *testing.T, payload []byte, label string) {
+	t.Helper()
+
+	var envelope robotEnvelope
+	if err := json.Unmarshal(payload, &envelope); err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("%s: invalid JSON: %v", label, err)
+	}
+	if envelope.Timestamp == "" {
+		t.Fatalf("%s: missing timestamp field", label)
+	}
+	if !envelope.Success {
+		t.Fatalf("%s: expected success=true", label)
+	}
+}
+
+func TestRobotBVAnalysisCommands(t *testing.T) {
+	testutil.RequireE2E(t)
+	testutil.RequireNTMBinary(t)
+	testutil.SkipIfBvUnavailable(t)
+
+	requireBVVersionAtLeast(t, tools.Version{Major: 0, Minor: 31, Patch: 0})
+
+	logger := testutil.NewTestLoggerStdout(t)
+	filePath := "internal/robot/robot.go"
+
+	cases := []struct {
+		name string
+		args []string
+	}{
+		{name: "robot-alerts", args: []string{"--robot-alerts"}},
+		{name: "robot-graph", args: []string{"--robot-graph"}},
+		{name: "robot-forecast", args: []string{"--robot-forecast=all"}},
+		{name: "robot-suggest", args: []string{"--robot-suggest"}},
+		{name: "robot-impact", args: []string{"--robot-impact=" + filePath}},
+		{name: "robot-search", args: []string{"--robot-search=triage"}},
+		{name: "robot-label-attention", args: []string{"--robot-label-attention"}},
+		{name: "robot-label-flow", args: []string{"--robot-label-flow"}},
+		{name: "robot-label-health", args: []string{"--robot-label-health"}},
+		{name: "robot-file-beads", args: []string{"--robot-file-beads=" + filePath}},
+		{name: "robot-file-hotspots", args: []string{"--robot-file-hotspots"}},
+		{name: "robot-file-relations", args: []string{"--robot-file-relations=" + filePath}},
+	}
+
+	for _, tc := range cases {
+		t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
+			logger.LogSection(fmt.Sprintf("BV %s", tc.name))
+			out := testutil.AssertCommandSuccess(t, logger, "ntm", tc.args...)
+			assertRobotEnvelope(t, out, tc.name)
+		})
+	}
+}
